nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Haye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Willi.

North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from a personal name, Frisian Hajo, ancient Germanic Haio; or from a short form of the female name Hadewig, composed of the elements hadu ‘battle, strife’ + wīg ‘war’.
German: variant of   Hey   4, 5.

variants.name: Deshayes
meanings: French: topographic name for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the plural of Old French haye ‘hedge’ (see Haye and Hayes ), with fused preposition and definite article des ‘from the’; or a habitational name denoting someone from Les Hayes, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Deshaies .

variants.name: Hayenga
meanings: East Frisian: patronymic from the personal name Hajo (see Haye 1). It is formed with the suffix -enga, a variant of -inga (from the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a).

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

variants.name: Hayen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse.  German Heinz, Wilhelm.
Flemish, East Frisian, and North German: patronymic from   Haye   1.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de la Haye was born in 1145, in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Ranulf de La Haye, was 25 and his mother, Adelaise de Essex, was 23. He had at least 1 daughter with Mrs Margaret De La Haye. He died in 1188, in his hometown, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de la Haye est né en 1145, à Canteburge, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Ranulf de La Haye, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Adelaise d’Essex, avait 23 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Mrs Margaret De La Haye. Il est décédé en 1188, à Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de la Haye 1145 in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ranulf de La Haye 25 und seine Mutter Adelaise de Essex 23. Er hatte mit Mrs Margaret De La Haye mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1188 im Alter von 43 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de la Haye è nato il nel 1145 nel Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Ranulf de La Haye, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Adelaise de Essex, aveva 23 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Mrs Margaret De La Haye. Morì nel 1188, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de la Hayeが1145年にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアカンブリッジで生まれた時，父Ranulf de La Hayeは25歳，母Adelaise de Essexは23歳であった。彼はMrs Margaret De La Hayeとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1188年に故郷で43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de la Haye(이)가 1145년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 캄브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ranulf de La Haye(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelaise de Essex(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Mrs Margaret De La Haye(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1188년 그의 고향에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de la Haye nasceu em 1145, em Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Ranulf de La Haye, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Adelaise de Essex, tinha 23 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Mrs Margaret De La Haye. Ele faleceu em 1188, em sua cidade natal, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de la Haye родился в 1145 г. в местности камбридж, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Ranulf de La Haye было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelaise de Essex, 23. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Mrs Margaret De La Haye. Он умер в 1188 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 43 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Geoffrey de la Haye nació en 1145, en Cantebriche, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Ranulf de La Haye, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Adelaise de Essex, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Mrs Margaret De La Haye. Murió en 1188, en Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años.
